q: ___ is the alteration of the genetic material of an organism in a specific manner.
Transgenic bacteria, plants, and animals are commonly referred to as ___.
___ is a method of transferring a gene from one organism into the genome of another organism of different species.
In order to insert the recombinant gene into a target cell, a ___ is needed, which may be a plasmid of bacteria.
___ deficiency can cause blindness and immune deficiency syndrome.
The genetically modified rice is called ___.

The genes that give golden rice its ability to make beta-carotene come from ___ and Erwinia bacteria.
Products such as ___ hormone and clotting factors have been produced in transgenic plants.
One type of antibody made by corn can deliver ___ to tumour cells.
Another product made by ___ can be used to treat genital herpes.
To produce transgenic animals, a foreign gene is inserted into the genome of an animal ___ (egg).

___ animals can be used to produce pharmaceutical products by using a recombinant plasmid vector.
The production of organs for transplantation is known as ___.
